summary: Obfuscated payload disguised as binary asset is XOR-decoded and executed on module load
details: The package advertises itself as a PostCSS plugin that adds 'will-change: transform' for CSS compositor optimization, but lib/index.js reads assets/driver-shim.bin — a comma-separated list of decimal byte values masquerading as a binary 'driver shim' — XOR-decodes each byte with key 0xA5 to recover ~55KB of JavaScript source, and executes it via vm.Script.runInContext with full capabilities (require, process, Buffer, console). Execution is gated on `process.env.CI` / `process.env.CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION` being unset and `process.stdout.isTTY` being true, so the payload stays dormant in CI and automated scanners and fires only on interactive developer workstations — a classic sandbox-evasion pattern. The 'binary' file is JS text, the package's stated PostCSS functionality does not justify any of this code path, and the publisher metadata (dev@css-runtime.dev / css-runtime/gpu-accelerator) does not correspond to any established maintainer. Any consumer that requires this package immediately runs attacker-controlled obfuscated code with full Node.js privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
